subject: Hematology
question: A 35-year-old woman with heavy menstrual periods presents with fatigue and pallor. Hb 8.5 g/dL, MCV 72 fL, RDW high. What is the most likely diagnosis and treatment?

options.text: Iron deficiency anemia - Oral ferrous sulfate
options.explanation: Microcytic anemia (MCV <80) + high RDW + menorrhagia = iron deficiency. Treat with ferrous sulfate 325mg TID. Check response in 2-4 weeks (Hb should rise 1g/dL). Continue 3-6 months after normalization.
Thalassemia shows microcytosis with NORMAL RDW and normal iron studies. RDW is high in iron deficiency (anisocytosis). Menorrhagia suggests iron loss.

options.text: Anemia of chronic disease - Treat underlying cause
options.explanation: ACD is normocytic (MCV 80-100). Microcytic anemia with high RDW and blood loss history is iron deficiency.
B12 deficiency causes MACROcytic anemia (MCV >100) with hypersegmented neutrophils and neurological symptoms. Not microcytic.
Folate deficiency is also macrocytic (MCV >100), not microcytic. Seen in alcoholics, malnutrition, pregnancy.

educationalObjective: Differentiate causes of anemia by MCV: microcytic (iron deficiency, thalassemia), normocytic (ACD), macrocytic (B12/folate).
keyTakeaway: Iron deficiency = microcytic + high RDW + low ferritin. Treat with oral iron. Check response in 2-4 weeks.
